# Thai Green Curry with Chicken
NOTE: This is an AI-generated test document, it's not real, and any resemblance to real people or places is coincidence.

Learned this one at a weekend cooking class at Marco's Kitchen downtown, taught by
Chef Anong. It's become the go-to weeknight dinner since it's faster than takeout.

## Ingredients
- 2 tbsp green curry paste (Mae Ploy brand, if you can find it)
- 1 can (14 oz) coconut milk
- 1.5 lbs chicken thighs, sliced thin
- 1 Thai eggplant, cubed (or regular eggplant in a pinch)
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 cup bamboo shoots
- 2 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 tbsp palm sugar
- Handful Thai basil leaves
- 2 kaffir lime leaves, torn
- Jasmine rice, for serving

## Instructions
1. Heat a splash of the thick coconut cream in a wok over medium-high heat until it separates.
2. Add curry paste and fry until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
3. Add chicken, stir to coat, cook until mostly opaque.
4. Pour in remaining coconut milk, bring to a simmer.
5. Add eggplant, bell pepper, and bamboo shoots. Simmer 10 minutes.
6. Stir in fish sauce, palm sugar, and lime leaves.
7. Finish with Thai basil right before serving over jasmine rice.

## Notes
Chef Anong recommended tasting as you go — the balance of salty, sweet, and spicy
should shift depending on the brand of curry paste. Marco's Kitchen sells the Mae Ploy
paste in their little pantry shop if the grocery store is out.
